author: runner-5
rationale: Input pin only (P2 P-9: PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholders are completed by ops amendment before any final-phase run; no design field or threshold is touched). The D-XIF held-out labelled extraction set (P10 sections 3-4; frozen iface_gate n>=300 per rung, failure Wilson-LB <= 0.10) now exists at data/d-xif/ and the 'PINNED-AT-INPUTS:d-xif' placeholder in pins.corpus_hashes.extraction-labelled-set resolves to its kot-corpus-hash/1 digest; a directly-recomputable key 'd-xif' is added for one-command verification via tools/registry/corpus-pin.py d-xif. CONTENT: 500 kernel-covered + 150 control REAL model outputs per rung (R1 = SmolLM2-135M-Instruct @ 12fd25f77366fa6b3b4b768ec3050bf629380bac, R2 = SmolLM2-360M-Instruct @ a10cc1512eabd3dde888204e902eca88bddb4951 — exactly the revisions pinned by amendment 2), generated 2026-07-08 by ONE Modal A10G run (poc/modal/modal_f2_xif.py, stamp 20260708-190006-modal-xif; provenance sidecar inside the hashed bytes) BEFORE any F2 final-phase run (DAG d-xif < f2.iface < f2.run). Per output, two decodes from the IDENTICAL P10 shared-affordance prompt: (1) the deployed IF-C surface (constrained sequence-logprob selection, the harness's own HFLM/extract_record/KernelVerifier machinery imported by the builder so the measured instrument is the deployed one) and (2) unconstrained greedy free decode (max 24 new tokens) for the IF-1 pilot. LABELLING IS FULLY MECHANICAL (no LLM, no human): extraction SUCCESS = a well-formed record was produced AND the deterministic verifier could decide it against the per-item record_sha256-pinned canonical record; the assembly script data/d-xif/build-dxif.py (inside the hashed bytes with the other builders) RE-DERIVES every label and every rate from the raw per-output records and fails closed on any mismatch with the runner summary. GATE MEASUREMENT (P10 section 4, frozen thresholds): extraction failures 0/500 at R1 and 0/500 at R2; one-sided 95% Wilson lower bound of the failure rate 0.0000 at both rungs (success-direction rendering: success Wilson-LB 0.9946 >= 0.90) => the extraction-failure instrument gate PASSES at both rungs with n=500 >= 300. Honest scope: the zero is largely a property of the IF-C surface (constrained selection cannot emit out-of-format answers); residual structural failure modes (malformed items, record-pin mismatches, unresolvable term labels, claim round-trip breaks) were measured as absent, not assumed. IF-1 FORK PILOT (P10 section 3, n=200 per rung, deciding measurement pre-declared at freeze): constrained-format tax h_degradation = -0.021 (one-sided 95% CI [-0.116, +0.074]) at R1 and -0.708 [-0.877, -0.545] at R2 — the constraint HELPS (strongly at R2, where unconstrained accuracy collapses to 0.115 vs 0.415 constrained and 106/200 free-decode outputs assert nothing); IF-A strict-parser F1 vs the mechanical reference annotation GOLD-ANN/1 = 0.824 (R1) / 0.919 (R2), below the 0.95 bar. NEITHER fork condition fires at EITHER rung => the pre-declared default STANDS: IF-C is the pinned model<->record interface for the F2 final phase (and per P10 the same choice carries to e9.reg). The fork decision, its statistics (paired item bootstrap, B=10000, seed 20260708) and the GOLD-ANN/1 caveat (mechanical reference annotation, not human gold; adequate because the gate needs no gold and the fork did not fire) are recorded verbatim inside the hashed bytes at data/d-xif/if1-fork.json and README.md. DETERMINISM: both decodes are greedy; the assembly is byte-stable (two build cycles verified identical); the only non-reproducible-by-construction bytes are the GPU inference outputs themselves, pinned by content hash + Modal provenance. Spend: ~0.14 GPU-h A10G (~$0.15 at Modal list) inside the frozen f2 usd_cap $60 / Tier-1 $80.
